danger_signals.visual_form: Roboticized entities, mechanical appearance contrasting organic world
danger_signals.meaning: Threat from Doctor Robotnik's forces; collision causes damage and Flicky loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Birds converted to mechanical shells, visually corrupted state
danger_signals.meaning: World state of occupation; Flickies are enslaved, world is under threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss encounters at end of acts; mechanical aesthetic in level design
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; danger escalates at zone climax

danger_signals.visual_form: Rings scatter and disappear; Flickies separate from Sonic
danger_signals.meaning: Health vulnerability; environmental hazards and enemies cause immediate consequence

danger_signals.visual_form: Themed zone-specific traps and pitfalls in isometric layout
danger_signals.meaning: World environment itself is hostile; requires navigation skill

safety_signals.visual_form: Television monitor sprites containing elemental shields
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary protection; visual safeguard item to break and collect
Temporary advantage; enables faster movement and evasion
Temporary invulnerability state; safe passage window
Health buffer; presence of rings = safety; absence = vulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: Life extension items visually distinct from rings
safety_signals.meaning: Extended survival capacity; world provides recovery resources

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC characters placed strategically in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Allies present in world; gateways to special stages and Chaos Emerald opportunity

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Safe zones where Sonic can operate; areas with ally NPCs

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Robotnik character design; mechanical robots throughout levels
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robot enemies visually derivative of Robotnik's design; zone-themed mechanical variants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mechanical aesthetic; roboticized Flickies; boss arena visual style; industrial/corrupted level design elements

faction_indicators.color_identity: Multi-colored (blue, pink, orange, green, red variants)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bird creatures; distinct color coding per behavioral type
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Roboticized Flickies appear as mechanical versions; behavioral variants by color indicate different response patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Caged/trapped Flickies indicate occupation state; free Flickies signal liberation progress; island setting as homeland

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character sprites as recognizable allies from series
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hidden within levels; presence signals safe access to Chaos Emerald opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mechanical aesthetic overlay on natural zones; roboticized Flickies; industrial elements corrupting themed environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Flickies flee or are enslaved; robots patrol; Robotnik controls zone climax battles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Flickies appear as mechanical robot variants; color-coded behavioral differences indicate loss of free will
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Flickies must be collected; different colors scatter/return differently; require rescue to restore to normal

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Zones cleared of occupation; visual transition as acts complete; Chaos Emeralds obtained signal restoration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Flickies increasingly freed; boss defeats reduce Robotnik's direct presence; special stage access indicates hope/capability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Island environment with unique visual identity; themed zones suggest otherworldly geography distinct from primary Sonic world
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Isolated setting creates contained conflict; rings as interdimensional travel mechanic hint at world crossing capability

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Chaos Emerald presence indicates power shift
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Special stages accessed through ally NPCs with 50+ rings; visual shift to behind-Sonic perspective during emerald pursuit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must achieve checkpoints; emerald collection unlocks final confrontation; true ending only possible with all seven

environmental_storytelling.example: Each zone visually distinct (isometric pre-rendered 3D converted to sprites), establishing world geography and creating narrative through environment traversal
Natural environment corrupted by mechanical aesthetic; Flickies' mechanical forms versus freed state tells occupation story without dialogue
Flicky colors (blue, pink, orange = loyal; green, red = unpredictable) communicate behavioral traits through visual design, teaching player mechanics through aesthetics
Shield, speed, and invincibility monitors scattered strategically signal safe zones or danger ahead; visual indicator of resource distribution
Robotnik encounters at act end with distinct visual arena; mechanical aesthetic emphasizes antagonist's corrupting influence over that zone
Camera transition from isometric to behind-Sonic perspective during Chaos Emerald pursuit visually signals narrative importance and world significance
Collision causes visual ring dispersal and Flicky separation, immediately communicating consequence and stakes without UI text

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Make effort to find and follow Sonic; loyal behavioral pattern indicates cooperative potential
Make effort to find and follow Sonic; loyal behavioral pattern
Make effort to find and follow Sonic; loyal behavioral pattern
Wander at random; unpredictable movement indicates less cooperative nature or disorientation from captivity
Wander at random and jump about; most difficult to recollect; indicates highest difficulty/resistance from captivity
Patrol and attack; defeat removes local threat; visual destruction communicates vulnerability of occupation force
Boss fight at zone climax; visual dominance in arena communicates primary threat; defeat progresses liberation narrative
With rings = protected; ringless = vulnerable; visual state communicates immediate threat level
Hidden within level; upon approach with 50+ rings, enable Chaos Emerald stage access; behavioral gatekeeping signals progression reward

notes: Sonic 3D Blast uses isometric perspective (2D sprites from pre-rendered 3D models) as primary visual language. Danger is communicated through enemy and obstacle density, visual robot corruption of world, and ring/Flicky scatter mechanics. Safety signals are power-up monitors and ring presence. Faction identity is clear: blue Sonic vs. red/mechanical Robotnik forces, with colored Flickies representing enslaved population. World state of occupation is visually manifest through mechanical aesthetic overlay on natural zones. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on visual contrast (organic vs. mechanical), color coding for behavioral information, and spatial progression through themed zones. No explicit text-based narrative is needed; world state is readable through visual manifestation and entity behavioral patterns.
